title: Time Reversal
summary: This page introduces time reversal for discrete-time signals, showing that the time-reversed version of x[n] is xr[n] = x[−n] and explaining the role of the origin as the fixed anchor point. It also presents Example 3.2 on obtaining x[k−n] through time reversal and shifting, along with drills on right-shift operations and sketching a time-reversed exponential sequence.
